title: Tangential flow filtration (TFF) concentration of phytoplankton
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1nerp","text":"Concentrate phytoplankton samples about 100-fold typically from 5L down to 20 mL.  Takes about 1 hour per sample.  Samples can be used for flow cytometry sorting or for cultures.  Enrichment by TFF usually keep growing for a longer time than unconcentrate samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f6a5a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9ec3","text":"Reference","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3u5e8","text":"Marie, D., Shi, X.L., Rigaut-Jalabert, F. \u0026 Vaulot, D. (2010). Use of flow cytometric sorting to better assess the diversity of small photosynthetic eukaryotes in the English Channel. FEMS Microbiology Ecology. 72. p.pp. 165–178.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":25},{"style":"bold","offset":228,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":229}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2A65987D8C3F4E07B4AC457F05FE2594","url":".https://www.researchgate.net/publication/42254387_Use_of_flow_cytometric_sorting_to_better_assess_the_diversity_of_small_photosynthetic_eukaryotes_in_the_English_Channel"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2jjcf","text":"In order to estimate actual concentration efficiency measure Synechoccus, pico, and nanoeularyote concentration before and after TFF.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"97pb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d0nod","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"bgme9","text":"
Necesssary equipement
•    Vivaflow Cartridge 100,000 MWCO (Regenerated Cellulose - RC) VF20C4  for viruses
•    Vivaflow Cartridge  0.2 μm (PES) VF20P7 for plankton
•    Masterflex Pump 6-600 rpm (ref Bioblock F39671) - It is critical to have a 600 rpm pump, lower speed will not work.  The rate of the pump must be adjustable.
•    Rotor 3 “galets” (ref  F39110)
•    1 Head high throughput (ref F40103) (Can be replaced with quick load head)
•    Replace tube provided by stronger tube with two connectors (see picture)
•    Bottle 6 L
•    Bottle 1 L
•    Conical tube 50 mL (Falcon tube)
•    Masterflex Tygon tubing size 16
•    Plastique pipettes (1 mL) - this is used to plunge in the sample
•    Clamps with screw (to control retentate speed)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2j2oc","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"35fgh","text":"Solutions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3o3pg","text":"MilliQ water : 1L","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"54ea4","text":"NaOH 0.1 N : 500 mL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c7ps5","text":"EtOJ 10% : 500 mL","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"67AA7D1F18714393AE954DD23B3B5CDB","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":null,"placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/qqchcw.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/qqchcw.jpg","width":null}}}}

steps: Get Vivaflow catridge out of storage
Mount Vivaflow catridge as Fig. 1 (image de C. Brussaard) in open circuit. a
Remove the clamps
Set the pump to maximum speed. Manometer should be at about 2.5 bars (with a new cassette sometimes the manometer get stuck, if the value is too low there is a leak in the system)
Rinse the cartridge with about 250 mL of MilliQ water (longer is cartridge has been stored in ethanol) undetermined 00:10:00 MilliQ water Contributed by users
Replace MilliQ water by sample in 6 L bottle
Take sample for flow cytometry to compute concentration factor.
Rinse cartridge with about 250 mL of sample
Put the retentate line into the sample bottle (Fig. 2B - Recirculation mode). Record sample volume and start pump increasing to macimum speed. a
Clamp the retentate line to increase filtrate flow so that Manometer gets up to 2.5 bars.
Concentrate sample until about 250 mL remains (6 L takes about one hour) 01:00:00
Transfer sample to a smaller bottle (250 mL) then finally to a 50 mL tube with conical bottom 00:10:00
Continue to concentrate very carefully, lowering the pump speed until the sample volume is reduced to 15-20 mL It is very important to lower the pump speed inonder to avoid loosing the sample.
When final volume is about 10 mL, clamp filtrate tube and recirculate slowly (no change of volume should take place)
Leaving the filtrate tube clamped, get the feed line out of the sample in order to get back the total volume of concentrated sample
Take sample for flow cytometry count (to compare with original sample concnetration and estimate concentration factor)
Store concentrated sample for later use (e.g. flow cytometry sorting, culture etc...)
Go back to Fig. 1 configuration (Open circuit)
Rinse 1 min with filtered sea water 00:01:00 Filtered Seawater (0.2 µm) Contributed by users
Rinse 1 min with distilled water 00:01:00 Distilled Water Contributed by users
Rinse with 50 mL NaOH 0.1 M undetermined 0.1 M NaOH Contributed by users
Put all three tubes (feed, retentate, filtrate) in bottle containing NaOH 0.1 M 0.1 M NaOH Contributed by users
Recirculate for 20 min (to get rid of everything on the cartridge filter) 00:20:00
Rinse with 250 mL of MilliQ water (Fig. 1) undetermined MilliQ water Contributed by users
Stop the pump and clamp all three tubes
Store at 4°C. For a storage beyond 1 day, store with Ethanol 10% Ethanol 10% Contributed by users
creator: Daniel Vaulot
